headline: Zocks Scales Privacy-First Agentic AI to 5,000 Advisory Firms After $45M Series B
the_so_what: Zocks reaching 5,000 firms — including enterprise clients Ameritas, Carson Group, Kestra, and Osaic — and using its $45M Series B to expand from task automation into revenue-generating advisor capabilities follows the same land-and-expand pattern that made Zoom and Slack sticky: solve the workflow problem first, then become the operating layer that is harder to remove than to renew.
summary: Zocks operates a privacy-first agentic AI platform that automates advisor workflows — including meeting preparation and notes, intake and account opening forms, tailored client emails, and document processing — and is already deployed across more than 5,000 firms including Ameritas, Carson Group, Kestra Financial, and Osaic. The platform's next phase, funded by this raise, will expand agentic AI capabilities, deepen enterprise integrations, and strengthen compliance controls specifically to help advisors surface new business opportunities — moving the capability beyond task automation toward revenue generation. The $45M Series B was co-led by Lightspeed Venture Partners and QED Investors, with participation from Illuminate Financial and existing backers including Motive Partners, founded in 2022 and headquartered in San Francisco.

key_stat.label: advisory firms currently using the Zocks AI platform

capability_evidence.evidence: Zocks is live across more than 5,000 firms — including named enterprise clients Ameritas, Carson Group, Kestra Financial, and Osaic — automating meeting notes, intake forms, client emails, and document processing.

landscape_context.current_maturity: Series B, deployed at scale across 5,000+ advisory firms with named enterprise clients
landscape_context.competitor_gap: No peer comparison data available for this capability in this segment

_enrichment.notes: Company not previously in landscape; no peer comparison data available for the advisor productivity segment, limiting competitive positioning assessment.

_format_errors: week "2026-03-23" is not the Monday of date "2026-01-27" (expected "2026-01-26")
